title: feat(orchestrator): migrate tab-sync hook off Yjs onto userTabsCollection

evidence.command: npx wrangler d1 migrations apply duraclaw-auth --local

evidence.step: Deep-link login (?session=X) → useTabSync.openTab → optimistic insert → server echo
evidence.result: D1 row persisted {id:'8a91aaef', sessionId:'29fa1ca6...', meta:'{"project":"baseplane"}'} and TabBar renders 'tab 29fa1ca6' after reload

evidence.step: Server-side insert → broadcastSyncedDelta → subscribeUserStream('user_tabs') → useLiveQuery reactively renders
evidence.method: POST a second tab via fetch; observe TabBar add 'tab draft:se' without reload
evidence.result: New tab visible within 2s; no reload required

evidence.step: Server-side delete → delta frame → useLiveQuery removes row
evidence.method: DELETE the second tab; observe TabBar remove it

evidence.step: No Yjs imports remain in use-tab-sync.ts
evidence.result: only a comment string 'shared Y.Doc' remains — no imports, no runtime use

scope_excluded: yjs / y-partyserver / y-indexeddb deps stay in package.json — still used by the above
followups: Users upgrading in place lose any open-tab state one time (Yjs IndexedDB → D1 is not auto-migrated). Acceptable one-time transition; not worth building a migrator for ephemeral tab pointers.

OPFS persistence hit NoModificationAllowedError during the first verify run (stale sync handle held by prior Chrome session) — fell back to memory-only storage. Pre-existing axi/dev-stack flakiness, unrelated to this cutover.
